Title: A glutamyl–tRNA reductase and its binding protein promote transitory starch biosynthesis and enhance grain quality and yield in rice

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: temporary carbon storage in leaves, source–sink carbon allocation, grain filling, grain quality and yield
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: constitutively expressed in multiple tissues, higher in panicles, leaves, and stems; not induced by carbon starvation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutively expressed in multiple tissues, higher in panicles, leaves, and stems; not induced by carbon starvation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: grain-filling stage; carbon starvation tested but no induction
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes leaf transitory starch and endosperm starch accumulation by stabilizing LSD3 and GBSSII and maintaining GBSSII activity; improves grain quality and yield
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GluTRBP physically interacts with LSD3 and GBSSII in chloroplasts. 2. CRISPR knockout of GluTRBP reduced leaf starch granules, total starch, amylose, and chlorophyll while increasing sucrose. 3. glutrbp mutants showed severe endosperm starch defects, floury/chalky grains, reduced grain thickness, grain weight, and yield. 4. GluTRBP delays degradation of LSD3 and GBSSII, and LSD3 also stabilizes GluTRBP. 5. GBSSII overexpression fully or partially rescued glutrbp defects, placing GluTRBP upstream of GBSSII.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; iodine staining; TEM/SEM; starch/amylose/sucrose quantification; Y2H; GST pull-down; BiFC; co-IP; subcellular localization; cell-free degradation assay; GBSSII overexpression rescue
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome analysis of flag leaves during grain filling; accession numbers NA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize Ubiquitin 1 promoter and 35S promoter used in interaction and rescue assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful upstream target to improve transitory starch biosynthesis, source–sink transport, grain quality, and yield, especially in combination with GBSSII; avoid knockout because it causes severe chlorophyll and grain defects
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of function causes pale-green leaves, strongly reduced starch, severe floury/chalky grains, reduced yield traits, and possible lethality in combination with lsd3
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a non-enzymatic binding/stabilizing factor rather than a catalytic enzyme or transcriptional regulator; unlike LSD3, not induced by carbon starvation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: leaf carbon storage and contribution to endosperm starch accumulation and grain quality
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: analyzed in leaves; transcript levels elevated in lsd3 and glutrbp mutants despite lower protein abundance; detailed tissue profile NA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: functions in leaves and endosperm; overexpression increased starch in both tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: key enzyme for amylose synthesis in leaves; promotes total starch and amylose accumulation in leaves and endosperm and affects grain chalkiness
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GluTRBP directly interacts with GBSSII, whereas LSD3 does not. 2. LSD3 and GluTRBP together maintain GBSSII protein stability and enzyme activity. 3. gbssII mutants showed reduced starch and amylose in leaves and endosperm, increased sucrose, and higher chalky-grain rate. 4. GBSSII overexpression partially rescued lsd3 and glutrbp mutant phenotypes. 5. GBSSII overexpression in wild type increased total starch in leaves and endosperm but also increased chalkiness and did not improve yield per plant because grain number per panicle decreased.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; immunoblotting; enzyme activity assay; Y2H; GST pull-down; BiFC; co-IP; starch/amylose/sucrose quantification; overexpression in mutant and wild-type backgrounds
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq on flag leaves during grain filling implicated starch/sucrose metabolism changes; accession numbers NA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize Ubiquitin 1 promoter for overexpression rescue; 35S promoter in some vector construction description
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for engineering starch accumulation and partial rescue of upstream module defects; however, constitutive overexpression may increase chalkiness and fail to improve yield, so tissue-specific or moderate expression strategies are advisable
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: overexpression increased chalky-grain rate and reduced grain number per panicle, offsetting gain in grain weight; knockout reduced starch quality traits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states GBSSII is the key enzyme responsible for amylose synthesis in leaves and also affects endosperm starch traits indirectly/experimentally; transcript abundance can increase while protein abundance and activity decrease in lsd3 and glutrbp mutants, indicating post-translational control

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: temporary carbon storage in leaves, source–sink carbon allocation, grain filling, grain quality and yield
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: tetrapyrrole/chlorophyll biosynthesis; upstream regulator of starch biosynthesis via GBSSII stabilization
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 5-aminolevulinic acid (ALA) formation step in tetrapyrrole pathway; transitory starch biosynthesis regulatory module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream in chlorophyll biosynthesis; upstream modulator of leaf amylose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes the NADPH-dependent reduction of glutamyl–tRNA to glutamate-1-semialdehyde, a precursor for 5-aminolevulinic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: rate-limiting enzyme in ALA/chlorophyll biosynthesis; in this paper acts as an upstream key control component for transitory starch biosynthesis through GBSSII stability
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GluTR-N domain; NADPH-binding domain; dimerization domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: constitutively expressed in all tested tissues, higher in roots, stems, and leaves; diurnal pattern with transcript levels decreasing during the day and increasing at night; induced by carbon starvation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutively expressed in all tested tissues, higher in roots, stems, and leaves; diurnal pattern; induced by carbon starvation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: diurnal light/dark cycle; carbon starvation; grain-filling stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes leaf transitory starch accumulation and amylose content, supports endosperm starch accumulation, carbon-source allocation to grains, grain quality, and yield
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LSD3 was cloned from the lsd3 mutant and encodes glutamyl–tRNA reductase. 2. Loss of LSD3 reduced leaf starch granules, total starch, amylose, and chlorophyll, and impaired carbon transport from leaves/stems to grains. 3. lsd3 caused lower endosperm starch and amylose, higher chalkiness and soluble sugar/protein, and reduced grain weight and yield. 4. LSD3 interacts with GluTRBP and together stabilizes GBSSII and maintains GBSSII activity. 5. GBSSII overexpression partially rescued lsd3 defects, placing LSD3 upstream of GBSSII.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping; Mut-Map; complementation with native genomic fragment; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; iodine staining; TEM/SEM; starch/amylose/sucrose quantification; subcellular localization; Y2H; GST pull-down; BiFC; co-IP; cell-free degradation assay; enzyme activity assay; GBSSII overexpression rescue
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome analysis of flag leaves during grain filling; whole-genome sequencing for Mut-Map on Illumina HiSeq PE150; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native LSD3 promoter for complementation; maize Ubiquitin 1 promoter and 35S promoter used in related transgene assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising target to improve transitory starch production, source–sink allocation, grain quality, and yield; likely best deployed with GBSSII-centered engineering or allele improvement rather than strong loss-of-function; caution because mutation causes pale-green leaves and yield penalty
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of function causes pale-green leaves, reduced chlorophyll, reduced 1000-grain weight and grain yield, and poor grain quality; possible lethality in complete double loss with GluTRBP was suggested
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although LSD3 is an enzyme in chlorophyll biosynthesis, the starch phenotype is mediated indirectly: LSD3 does not directly bind GBSSII but acts with GluTRBP to stabilize GBSSII; EMS allele causes L484F substitution in the dimerization domain
